title: myosotis arvensis subsp. rifana
intro: Myosotis arvensis subsp. rifana, commonly known as Rif Mountain Forget-me-not, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the borage family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Rif Mountain Forget-me-not are often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications to harness its medicinal and health-promoting properties.
